2603.18256.title: MolRGen: A Training and Evaluation Setting for De Novo Molecular Generation with Reasonning Models
2603.18256.paper_summary: Recent advances in reasoning-based large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated substantial improvements in complex problem-solving tasks. Motivated by these advances, several works have explored the application of reasoning LLMs to drug discovery and molecular design. However, most existing approaches either focus on evaluation or rely on training setups that require ground-truth labels, such as molecule pairs with known property modifications. Such supervision is unavailable in \textit{de novo} molecular generation, where the objective is to generate novel molecules that optimize a desirability score without prior knowledge of high-scoring candidates. To bridge this gap, we introduce MolRGen, a large-scale benchmark and dataset for training and evaluating reasoning-based LLMs on \textit{de novo} molecular generation. Our contributions are threefold. First, we propose a setting to evaluate and train models for \textit{de novo} molecular generation and property prediction. Second, we introduce a novel diversity-aware top-$k$ score that captures both the quality and diversity of generated molecules. Third, we show our setting can be used to train LLMs for molecular generation, training a 24B LLM with reinforcement learning, and we provide a detailed analysis of its performance and limitations.
2603.18256.paper_summary_zh: 最近在基於推理的大型語言模型（LLMs）方面的進展顯示出在複雜問題解決任務中有顯著的改善。受到這些進展的激勵，幾項研究探索了將推理 LLM 應用於藥物發現和分子設計的可能性。然而，大多數現有的方法要麼專注於評估，要麼依賴於需要真實標籤的訓練設置，例如具有已知性質修改的分子對。在\textit{de novo} 分子生成中，這種監督是不可用的，因為其目標是生成新穎的分子，以優化一個期望分數，而不需要對高分候選者的先驗知識。為了填補這一空白，我們介紹了 MolRGen，一個用於訓練和評估基於推理的 LLM 在\textit{de novo} 分子生成上的大型基準和數據集。我們的貢獻有三個方面。首先，我們提出了一個設置來評估和訓練用於\textit{de novo} 分子生成和性質預測的模型。其次，我們引入了一種新穎的考慮多樣性的 top-$k$ 分數，該分數同時捕捉生成分子的質量和多樣性。第三，我們展示了我們的設置可以用於訓練 LLM 進行分子生成，並使用強化學習訓練了一個 24B 的 LLM，並提供了其性能和限制的詳細分析。
2603.18256.author: Philippe Formont et.al.
2603.18256.authors: Philippe Formont, Maxime Darrin, Ismail Ben Ayed, Pablo Piantanida
